rulings.Case Title: GLORIA TRAN VS. BAYVIEW CAPITAL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 17, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANT MARIANN FINEGOLD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant Feingold¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted in its entirety. Defendant shifted the burden in it moving papers by showing that one or more elements of Plaintiff¿s cause of action for fraud could not be established. Specifically, Defendant shows that plaintiff cannot establish a knowing misrepresentation, intent to induce reliance, or justifiable reliance. The Court does not consider the opposition of Plaintiff filed on April 27th because it was untimely and failed to provide a separate statement. See CCP§437c(b)(3); Lewis v. County of Sacramento (2001) 93 Cal.App.107, 115. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA TRAN VS. BAYVIEW CAPITAL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Shirley Cervantes' Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 17, 2011, line 1, Defendant Shirley Cervantes' Motion For Summary Judgment. Defendants Gujral Family Foundation and Cervantes¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to the 1st and 2nd causes of action. Defendants have shifted the burden to plaintiff by showing that one or more elements of Plaintiff¿s cause of action for fraud could not be established. Specifically, Defendant shows that Plaintiff cannot establish a knowing misrepresentation. Defendants¿ Motion for Summary Adjudication as to the 5th and 6th causes of action is denied. Plaintiff has raised triable issues of material fact as to whether the interest calculation was correct. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID ALUF et al VS. DAVID BANKS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Filing Of Satisfaction Of Judgment And For Sanctions/Damages
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 17, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANTS DAVID BANKS, RUBY DOMINGUEZ, LEE BANKS Motion To Compel Filing Of Satisfaction Of Judgment And For Sanctions/Damages. MOTION TO COMPEL FILING OF SATISFACTION OF JUDGMENT AND SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF HAS EXECUTED THE SATISFACTION OF JUDGMENT WHICH RENDERS THIS ISSUE MOOT. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF WAS REQUIRED TO DO SO AFTER JUDGE ALVARADO DENIED THE MOTION FOR PREJUDGMENT INTEREST/NEW TRIAL. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO DO SO UNTIL THIS MOTION WAS FILED. THEREFORE, THE COURT AWARDS SANCTIONS TO DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,140.00. PLAINTIFF MUST REMIT THIS AMOUNT TO DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL BY JULY 15, 2011. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: DAMIAN FLEMING VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 17, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendants¿ Motion for Summary Adjudication/Summary Judgment is granted in part and denied in part. As to the Second cause of action (Violation of Labor Code §1102.5) the motion for Summary Adjudication is granted. Plaintiff cannot show that he had a reasonable belief that Defendant was violating Federal, State of local law when he complained about the use of pesticides. There is not evidence to support Plaintiff¿s contention that the manner in which the subject pesticides were used violated any applicable law or that there is any applicable laws governing the spraying or that Defendant violated any such laws. Moreover, Plaintiff cannot support a claim that he knew that his complaint was disclosing a violation of such laws or that he reasonably believe that it did. As to the First cause of action (Violation of Labor Code §6310, the motion for Summary Adjudication is denied. Plaintiff has raised a triable issue of material fact regarding whether or not the Defendant¿s employment decision was based on his complaints to his supervisor about the spraying of pesticide spraying. While the final decision was made by the Department Head, Phil Ginsburg, that decision was based upon performance information passed up the chain of command at the Department beginning with Plaintiff¿s Supervisor to whom Plaintiff made his complaints. Moreover, some of the performance issues raised to Mr. Ginsburg were not raised in Plaintiff¿s written or oral performance evaluations. While such evidence is circumstantial, it is sufficient to permit a jury to infer that Plaintiff¿s Supervisor raised these issues in retaliation for Plaintiff¿s complaints about the pesticides. Clark v. Claremont University Center, (1992) 6 Cal. App.4th 667-60. As to the Third cause of action (Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress), the motion for Summary Adjudication is gran

rulings.Case Title: FOUNDERS REALTY, INC. VS. MARIA IRMA LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Pedro Padilla, Gabriella Ocampo Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 17, 2011, line 14, Defendants Pedro Padilla, Gabriella Ocampo Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint. DEMURRER OVERRULED. 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CLAIM SEEKING A DECLARATION THAT ENFORCEMENT OF THE LEASES AGAINST PLAINTIFF WOULD CONSTITUTE AN UNCONSTITUTIONAL TAKING. THERE ARE NO ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES THAT PLAINTIFF WAS ENTITLED TO EXHAUST. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: DANIELLE NICOLOSI-MALMQUIST VS. MEREDITH TRESI-MALMQUIST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 17, 2011, line 6, DEFENDANT MEREDITH MALMQUIST'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT WAS PROPERLY SERVED. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT HAS MADE ANY PURPOSEFUL CONTACT WITH PERSONS IN CALFORNIA OR HAS MINIMUM CONTACT WITH CALIFORNIA THAT WOULD RENDER DEFENDANT SUBJECT TO THIS COURT'S JURISDICTION. =(302/LMG)
